title: Question: time units for vcd files.
body:
---


Author Name: **Dennis Muhlestein**
Original Redmine Message: 1734 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I see the default when I dump main_time to the vcd file is 1ns.

 tfp->dump(main_time) -> produces vcd file where each dump is 1ns.

I see in the VerilatedVcd internal class there is support for set_time_unit but I'm not quickly seeing anywhere that is actually used.  
You're supposed to use VerilatedVcd[S]C for actual usage so those methods aren't exposed anyway.  

So.... if I want to change the timescale to something else I seem to be missing something.

I realize of course that I can interpret main_time however I want and ignore the timescale in the vcd output but it's pretty nice for comparing with other sims to make sure things are working right of course.

Another thing I thought of, which I don't know if will work

suppose I wanted ps resolution so I just dump main_time/1000?

 tfp->dump(main_time/1000)

The only issue here is I see main_time gets casted to a uint64 in the dump header.  Haven't experimented much with what the actual output looks like.
